overview: Terra, Herald of Hope is a spell-synergy engine that generates a relentless swarm of flying Spirits with every spell cast. The deck aims to out-value opponents through card advantage, recursive threats, and resilient board presence, winning by overwhelming the board with evasive tokens or replaying Terra via recursion to close out games.
win_conditions: Reanimating Terra to cast multiple spells in a single turn, generating a lethal swarm.
Also, direct damage spells or combat with flying tokens when opponents lack blockers.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including basics for B/R/W), Arcane Signet, and a turn-1 or turn-2 play.
Prioritize hands with 3+ lands if they include a turn-2 play.
mulligan_criteria: Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, or 4+ lands with no early impact or mana fixing.

early_game: Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip or disruption if available.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Terra if mana allows, or play a cheap spell to start the token engine.
If holding Terra, consider playing a cheap spell first to generate a token, then cast Terra to maximize triggers on your opponent's turn.
mid_game: Keep mana flexible for instant-speed responses and casting spells to fuel Terra.
Don't overcommit to the board if you can't protect it.
keep Terra or key threats in hand if the graveyard is unsafe.
Commit threats when you have removal or a board wipe ready.
Hold back if opponents have sweepers or targeted hate.
Use flying tokens to trade efficiently against non-flying blockers and preserve your key creatures.
late_game: Reanimating Terra to cast multiple spells in a single turn, generating a lethal swarm.
Also, direct damage spells or combat with flying tokens when opponents lack blockers.
When you have graveyard protection, enough mana to cast multiple spells, and opponents lack sweepers.
Also when you can close the game in 1-2 turns with a clear path to combat or spell triggers.

sequencing_tips: Terra's ability triggers on any spell, including instants and sorceries, so use cheap cantrips and removal to fuel it.
Reanimate is a key win-con but vulnerable to graveyard hate
have backup plans like direct damage or combat.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without protection.
keep a hand ready to rebuild.
Don't forget to use Terra's activated ability to bounce tokens for card draw when you're low on resources.

matchups.advice: Use cheap spells, cantrips, and instant-speed recursion. Cast Terra after clearing the stack or with protection. Bait counters with low-value spells before committing your engine.
Apply early pressure, disrupt mana sources, and use removal on key ramp creatures. Don't let them get too far ahead; force them to interact with your board before they can stabilize.
Use flying tokens to block, spot removal on key attackers, and stabilize with board wipes. Don't overextend into combat; prioritize survival and out-value them in the late game.
Out-value them with spell density, use recursion to bypass graveyard hate, and pressure with flying tokens. Force them to use resources on multiple threats rather than focusing on one.
Win the resource war. Prioritize graveyard protection, use targeted removal on opponent's Terra, and out-cast them with better card draw. Control the stack and force unfavorable trades.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes, targeted removal on Terra, and graveyard recursion.
Note when opponents save removal for your key threats.
Track graveyard contents for Reanimate targets and be aware of opponent's mana development.
